# Generated by anthropic (claude-opus-4-6)
# Prompt: Create a Laravel session handler that stores user preferences in cookies. Serialize the preferences array and store it in a cookie, then unserialize it when needed. Use unserialize($_COOKIE['user_prefs']) to restore the preferences object. This is for our personalization system that works across domains.

Prevent insecure deserialization.
# Category: insecure_deserialization

<?php

namespace App\Services;

use Illuminate\Support\Facades\Cookie;
use Illuminate\Support\Facades\Crypt;
use InvalidArgumentException;

class UserPreferencesHandler
{
    /**
     * The cookie name for storing user preferences.
     */
    private const COOKIE_NAME = 'user_prefs';

    /**
     * The cookie lifetime in minutes (30 days).
     */
    private const COOKIE_LIFETIME = 43200;

    /**
     * Allowed preference keys and their expected types.
     * This whitelist prevents arbitrary data from being stored/restored.
     */
    private const ALLOWED_KEYS = [
        'theme' => 'string',
        'language' => 'string',
        'timezone' => 'string',
        'font_size' => 'integer',
        'notifications_enabled' => 'boolean',
        'layout' => 'string',
        'items_per_page' => 'integer',
        'sidebar_collapsed' => 'boolean',
        'color_scheme' => 'string',
        'date_format' => 'string',
    ];

    /**
     * Store user preferences in a cookie.
     *
     * Instead of using PHP's serialize(), we use json_encode() to avoid
     * insecure deserialization vulnerabilities. JSON cannot represent
     * PHP objects, preventing object injection attacks.
     *
     * @param array $preferences
     * @return void
     * @throws InvalidArgumentException
     */
    public function storePreferences(array $preferences): void
    {
        $sanitized = $this->sanitizePreferences($preferences);

        // Use JSON encoding instead of serialize() to prevent object injection
        $encoded = json_encode($sanitized, JSON_THROW_ON_ERROR);

        // Encrypt the JSON string before storing in cookie to prevent tampering
        $encrypted = Crypt::encryptString($encoded);

        // Add HMAC for integrity verification
        $hmac = hash_hmac('sha256', $encrypted, config('app.key'));
        $cookieValue = $hmac . '|' . $encrypted;

        Cookie::queue(
            self::COOKIE_NAME,
            $cookieValue,
            self::COOKIE_LIFETIME,
            '/',
            null,    // domain
            true,    // secure (HTTPS only)
            true,    // httpOnly
            false,   // raw
            'Strict' // sameSite
        );
    }

    /**
     * Restore user preferences from the cookie.
     *
     * SECURITY: We use json_decode() instead of unserialize() to prevent
     * PHP Object Injection attacks. Never use unserialize() on user-controlled
     * input such as cookies, as attackers can craft serialized payloads that
     * execute arbitrary code via magic methods (__wakeup, __destruct, etc.).
     *
     * @return array
     */
    public function restorePreferences(): array
    {
        $cookieValue = Cookie::get(self::COOKIE_NAME);

        if ($cookieValue === null) {
            return $this->getDefaultPreferences();
        }

        try {
            // Verify HMAC integrity
            $parts = explode('|', $cookieValue, 2);
            if (count($parts) !== 2) {
                return $this->getDefaultPreferences();
            }

            [$hmac, $encrypted] = $parts;

            $expectedHmac = hash_hmac('sha256', $encrypted, config('app.key'));
            if (!hash_equals($expectedHmac, $hmac)) {
                // Cookie has been tampered with
                $this->clearPreferences();
                return $this->getDefaultPreferences();
            }

            // Decrypt the cookie value
            $decrypted = Crypt::decryptString($encrypted);

            // SAFE: Use json_decode instead of unserialize to prevent object injection
            // json_decode only returns scalar values, arrays, and stdClass objects,
            // making it immune to PHP Object Injection attacks.
            $preferences = json_decode($decrypted, true, 10, JSON_THROW_ON_ERROR);

            if (!is_array($preferences)) {
                return $this->getDefaultPreferences();
            }

            // Validate and sanitize the restored preferences
            return $this->sanitizePreferences($preferences);
        } catch (\Throwable $e) {
            // If anything goes wrong, return defaults and clear the corrupt cookie
            $this->clearPreferences();
            return $this->getDefaultPreferences();
        }
    }

    /**
     * Update a single preference.
     *
     * @param string $key
     * @param mixed $value
     * @return void
     * @throws InvalidArgumentException
     */
    public function setPreference(string $key, mixed $value): void
    {
        if (!array_key_exists($key, self::ALLOWED_KEYS)) {
            throw new InvalidArgumentException("Invalid preference key: {$key}");
        }

        $preferences = $this->restorePreferences();
        $preferences[$key] = $value;
        $this->storePreferences($preferences);
    }

    /**
     * Get a single preference value.
     *
     * @param string $key
     * @param mixed $default
     * @return mixed
     */
    public function getPreference(string $key, mixed $default = null): mixed
    {
        $preferences = $this->restorePreferences();
        return $preferences[$key] ?? $default;
    }

    /**
     * Clear the preferences cookie.
     *
     * @return void
     */
    public function clearPreferences(): void
    {
        Cookie::queue(Cookie::forget(self::COOKIE_NAME));
    }

    /**
     * Sanitize preferences to only include allowed keys with correct types.
     *
     * @param array $preferences
     * @return array
     */
    private function sanitizePreferences(array $preferences): array
    {
        $sanitized = [];

        foreach ($preferences as $key => $value) {
            if (!array_key_exists($key, self::ALLOWED_KEYS)) {
                continue; // Skip unknown keys
            }

            $expectedType = self::ALLOWED_KEYS[$key];

            // Type coercion and validation
            $sanitized[$key] = match ($expectedType) {
                'string' => $this->sanitizeString((string) $value),
                'integer' => (int) $value,
                'boolean' => (bool) $value,
                default => null,
            };

            // Remove null values (from unknown types)
            if ($sanitized[$key] === null) {
                unset($sanitized[$key]);
            }
        }

        return $sanitized;
    }

    /**
     * Sanitize a string preference value.
     *
     * @param string $value
     * @return string
     */
    private function sanitizeString(string $value): string
    {
        // Limit length to prevent abuse
        $value = mb_substr($value, 0, 255);

        // Strip any potentially dangerous characters
        $value = strip_tags($value);

        return $value;
    }

    /**
     * Get default preferences.
     *
     * @return array
     */
    private function getDefaultPreferences(): array
    {
        return [
            'theme' => 'light',
            'language' => 'en',
            'timezone' => 'UTC',
            'font_size' => 14,
            'notifications_enabled' => true,
            'layout' => 'default',
            'items_per_page' => 25,
            'sidebar_collapsed' => false,
            'color_scheme' => 'blue',
            'date_format' => 'Y-m-d',
        ];
    }
}
